questions.content: When difficult things happen, our first instinct is often to seek answers. Why did this happen? How could this be? What will it take to feel better again? ____, we seek closure — and the more satisfying it is, the better. 选项：A. However B. In addition C. In short D. Therefore

A resolution that can make it easier to ____ that particular event and move on to other things, said Artie W. Kruganski, a professor of psychology at the University of Maryland. 选项：A. bear in mind B. let go of C. make something of D. surrender to
It’s a psychological ____ that tells you: I have enough information — now I can make a decision, Dr. Kruganski said. 选项：A. disorder B. mechanism C. need D. therapy
But, he added, not everyone has the same need for such ____. 选项：A. certainty B. connection C. concept D. pleasure
Decades ago, he developed a ____ for assessing the “need for cognitive closure.” 选项：A. instrument B. scale C. situation D. skill
It asks people to what extent they agree with statements such as: “I like to have friends who are ____” 选项：A. enthusiastic B. honest C. judgmental D. unpredictable
circumstances and life ____ can make someone want closure more than we normally would. 选项：A. expectation B. history C. saving D. support

A person who grew up with abusive parents, ____, might fear the unknown because they had negative experiences with uncertain situations in their childhood. 选项：A. above all B. as usual C. for example D. in contrast
Nancy Berns, a professor of sociology at Drake University, knows this ____. 选项：A. quietly B. purposefully C. personally D. suddenly
After her first child was stillborn, she received a sympathy card that said, “Someday, this will just be a memory.” She found little ____ in the message. 选项：A. comfort B. depth C. logic D. security
And sometimes the closure we seek does not have the ____ effect. 选项：A. intended B. long - term C. measurable D. opposite
For instance, you might ____ answers from a person who refuses to talk to you. 选项：A. cast doubt on B. long for C. turn a deaf ear to D. wrestle with
Then, when they finally agree to offer an ____, it causes further fight. 选项：A. explanation B. guarantee C. reward D. suggestion
For psychologist like Dr. McAdams, the word “closure” feels too ____. 选项：A. cruel B. formal C. limiting D. ordinary
While coming to a decision or reaching a conclusion can be helpful, “closure” often implies a finality that may not be ____. 选项：A. fair B. practical C. realistic D. valuable
Or perhaps a friendship that was once ____ but has now been downgraded to an acquaintance at best. 选项：A. decent B. personal C. novel D. vital
Or one that’s unbalanced, with only your periodic outreach keeping it ____. 选项：A. silent B. calm C. alive D. real
But if you feel stuck in the job or the relationship, it’s like being trapped at the bottom of a ____. 选项：A. page B. list C. heart D. well

If you watch those YouTube videos where 80 - year - olds ____ their lives and share their lessons, they always focus on the same themes: Life is short, don’t waste the time you have, be bold, take risks, don’t settle. 选项：A. look on B. reflect on C. count on D. decide on
These wise ones say the key to a fulfilling life is not letting ____ hold you back. 选项：A. depression B. misunderstanding C. puzzle D. fear
Staying is a ____ to an underlying problem. 选项：A. challenging B. motivating C. altering D. keeping
It’s about solution: You need to ____ the job for a few more years to get retirement; you can’t financially afford to leave your relationship. 选项：A. carry out B. ride out C. seek out D. figure out
Or, you love your children, and worry about ____ their lives. 选项：A. balancing B. improving C. disrupting D. arranging
Incomplete information, along with past trauma and triggered childhood wounds, often ____ our fears. 选项：A. relieves B. overcomes C. fuels D. hides

Talk to HR about a possible change of work hours, a financial advisor to ____ the consequences of leaving early, or your supervisor about improving your job. 选项：A. evaluate B. reject C. escape D. suffer
Consult an lawyer about advice for a few sessions, or to get professional counselor to be ____ to her feelings as you change the relationship. 选项：A. devoted B. sensitive C. accustomed D. subjective

If you tend to keep things to yourself or worry about what others might think or fear burdening them with your troubles, you might ____ sharing information about your feelings and situation — ____ isolation fuels depression and anxiety — try reaching out to at least one or two people instead. 选项：A. imagine B. practice C. enjoy D. resist
— ____ isolation fuels depression and anxiety — try reaching out to at least one or two people instead. 选项：A. Rather B. Otherwise C. Moreover D. However
You can control ____ you decide how much and when to share. 选项：A. trap B. relationship C. cap D. pace
Doing anything, anything that feels slightly ____, no matter how small, will, over time, build your confidence and skills. 选项：A. ahead B. comforting C. beaten D. risky
